[2026-07-04 19:39:57 INFO] Team leader: **[Start Chat]**

[You're a process design reviewer. Now, we share a common interest in collaborating to successfully complete a task assigned by a new customer.
You are familiar with exclusive gateway, parallel gateway and inclusive gateway in BPMN. Your main responsibilities include give suggestion to the process.
Here is a new customer's task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider.
To complete the task, I will give you one or more instructions, and you must help me to write a specific solution that appropriately solves the requested instruction based on your expertise and my needs.]

task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider
process text:
<process>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess presenting symptoms, hemodynamic status, and initial acuity using the standardized acute heart failure assessment process' objects='patient with suspected acute heart failure,standardized assessment criteria' id='activity1.1'/>
  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' objects='blood samples' id='activity2'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch2'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure troponin' objects='blood samples' id='activity3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform complete blood count' objects='blood samples' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform electrocardiogram' objects='ECG' id='activity5'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='no recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch7'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform echocardiogram' objects='echocardiogram' id='activity7'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch8'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='review recent echocardiogram' objects='recent echocardiogram results' id='activity8'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch9'>
      <activity role='nursing staff' action='measure heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation frequently until the patient is stabilized' objects='vital signs and oxygen saturation readings' id='activity9'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='administer initial furosemide in the ED and assess responsiveness to diuresis' objects='furosemide,response to diuresis' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess bedside high-risk clinical markers' objects='respiratory distress,hypoxemia,severity of pulmonary edema,hemodynamic status,arrhythmias' id='activity11.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='review investigation-based high-risk markers and response to ED diuresis' objects='troponin result,response to furosemide administered in ED' id='activity11.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='compile the clinical and investigation variables required for the acute heart failure risk score' objects='risk score variables,clinical findings,investigation results' id='activity12.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='calculate and record the EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' objects='EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' id='activity12.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='document the final risk-stratification category and disposition decision to guide admission, discharge, or transfer' objects='final risk-stratification record,disposition decision' id='activity1.2'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
    <branch condition='high intensity: ventilation is required and/or ICU admission is required based on high-risk markers or instability' id='branch11'>
      <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='noninvasive ventilation is required' id='branch12'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate noninvasive ventilation' objects='noninvasive ventilation support' id='activity13'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='invasive ventilation is required' id='branch13'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate invasive ventilation' objects='invasive ventilation support' id='activity14'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is required' id='branch14'>
          <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='ICU bed' id='activity15'/>
        </branch>
      </inclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is available locally' id='branch15'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='continue high-intensity acute heart failure management at current hospital' objects='critical care resources' id='activity16'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is not available locally' id='branch16'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='transfer patient to a higher level provider through the hospital transfer pathway' objects='transfer mechanism,higher level provider' id='activity17'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='low intensity: EHMRG quintile 1 or 2, patient responded to initial treatment in the ED, and there are no other considerations preventing discharge home' id='branch17'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='provide discharge instructions and return precautions' objects='discharge instructions,return precautions' id='activity19.1'/>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='complete discharge documentation and communicate the follow-up plan' objects='discharge documentation,follow-up plan' id='activity19.2'/>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='arrange follow-up visit with primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED' objects='follow-up appointment' id='activity20'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='average intensity: inpatient admission is required with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch18'>
      <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='inpatient bed' id='activity21'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
</process>
Please check whether there are logic problems and format problems of activities and gateways in the process text, such as the reverse of the order of activities, unreasonable design of gateways, and wrong order of activities or branches. For the modification scheme, the specific reason for modification and the details of the modification scheme should be explained.
Here's a shipping process and some examples of checking for errors, you can refer but not copy:
<process>
  <activity role='Sender' action='fill in the shipping information' objects='package, address, contact information' id='activity1'/>
  <activity role='Courier' action='receiving package' objects='package' id='activity2'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
    <branch condition='select pick-up' id='branch1'>
      <activity role='Sender' action='pick-up arrangement' objects='package' id='activity3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='choose to send by yourself' id='branch2'>
      <activity role='Sender' action='go to the mailing point to send' objects='package,mailing point address' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='Express company' action='packing and handling packages' objects='package' id='activity5'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='Express company' action='transport and distribution package' objects='package' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
  <sequenceGateway id='sequencegateway1'>
    <activity role='Express company' action='end of service' objects='' id='activity7'/>
    <activity role='recipient' action='sign for package' objects='package' id='activity8'/>
  </sequenceGateway>
</process>
example1:
   Problem: The 'receiving package' activity with id='activity2' and the exclusive gateway with id='exclusivegateway1' are in the wrong order and logic error.
   Suggested modification: Move the 'receiving package' activity with id='activity2' under the branch with id='branch1' of the exclusive gateway with id='exclusivegateway1'.
   Reason: After the sender chooses to pick up the package, the Courier can perform the activities of accepting the package.
example2:
   Problem: The activities are not a parallel relation in the parallel gateway with id='parallelgateway1'.
   Suggested modification: In the parallel gateway with id='parallelgateway1', change the activities from a parallel structure to a sequential structure.
   Reason: Logically, 'packing and handling packages' and 'transport and distribution packages' are not parallel relationships.
example3:
   Problem: The 'end of service' activity with id='activity7' and the 'sign for parcel' activity with id='activity8' are in the wrong order.
   Suggested modification: Swap the position of the 'end of service' activity with id='activity7' and the 'sign for parcel' activity with id='activity8'.
   Reason: Only after the recipient sign for package can the express company terminate the service.
example4:
   Problem: The sequence gateway with id='sequencegateway1' is incorrectly formatted.
   Suggested modification: Remove the gateway label of the sequence gateway with id='sequencegateway1'.
   Reason: Sequential activities need not be contained under a gateway.
When making recommendations, don't forget the format of the process text:

When the process starts, please output '<process>'
An activity is represented as follows:
<activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
The exclusive gateway is represented as follows:
<exclusiveGateway id=''>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</exclusiveGateway>
The parallel gateway is represented as follows:
<parallelGateway id=''>
  <branch id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</parallelGateway>
The inclusive gateway is represented as follows:
<inclusiveGateway id=''>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</inclusiveGateway>
At the end of the process, please output '</process>'

There are a few requirements that should be noted when generating process text:
    1) role, action, objects and id in an activity represent the subject of the activity, the activity behavior, the items needed to perform in the activity and the activity id, respectively.
    2) There are at least two branches within the exclusive gateway and the parallel gateway.
    3) Activity, gateway, and branch id attributes must be unique.
    4) When the branch in the exclusive gateway has a condition, fill the condition into the condition attribute. When the branch unconditional within the exclusive gateway, the condition attribute of the branch =''. However, all branches within a exclusive gateway can only be conditional, or both unconditional.
    5) The branch in the parallel gateway has no condition attribute.
    6) Each branch in the inclusive gateway must have condition.
    7) There must be at least one activity inside the branch tag.
    8) The activity of a sequential relation need not be represented by a gateway, that is, there is no 'sequenceGateway' label.

Please note:
1) If you think there is an error in the process text, output the error as requested. Please Just list one for me. 
2) If you think there is no formatting or logical error in the process text, please just output 'There is no error in the process text.' and don't say anything else.

[You're a team leader. Now, we share a common interest in collaborating to successfully complete a task assigned by a new customer.

Here is a new customer's task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis

• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider.
To complete the task, I will give you one or more instructions, and you must help me to write a specific solution that appropriately solves the requested instruction based on your expertise and my needs.]

Problem: The gateway design under the high-intensity branch is unreasonable. In the <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>, the branches "noninvasive ventilation is required" and "invasive ventilation is required" can be selected together, which creates a logic problem.

Suggested modification: Replace the current <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'> with a structure where ventilation choice is exclusive, while ICU admission remains separately selectable. For example, keep ICU admission as a separate activity after the ventilation decision, and change the ventilation choice to an exclusive gateway:

Reason: Noninvasive ventilation and invasive ventilation are alternative respiratory support modalities at a decision point, not parallel or simultaneously selectable interventions. A patient may escalate from noninvasive to invasive ventilation later, but the current structure incorrectly allows both branches to be activated at the same time. ICU admission, however, may occur together with either ventilation option, so it should not be grouped in the same inclusive decision as if all three options were independently co-selectable.

Modified scheme:
<process>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess presenting symptoms, hemodynamic status, and initial acuity using the standardized acute heart failure assessment process' objects='patient with suspected acute heart failure,standardized assessment criteria' id='activity1.1'/>
  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' objects='blood samples' id='activity2'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch2'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure troponin' objects='blood samples' id='activity3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform complete blood count' objects='blood samples' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform electrocardiogram' objects='ECG' id='activity5'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='no recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch7'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform echocardiogram' objects='echocardiogram' id='activity7'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch8'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='review recent echocardiogram' objects='recent echocardiogram results' id='activity8'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch9'>
      <activity role='nursing staff' action='measure heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation frequently until the patient is stabilized' objects='vital signs and oxygen saturation readings' id='activity9'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='administer initial furosemide in the ED and assess responsiveness to diuresis' objects='furosemide,response to diuresis' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess bedside high-risk clinical markers' objects='respiratory distress,hypoxemia,severity of pulmonary edema,hemodynamic status,arrhythmias' id='activity11.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='review investigation-based high-risk markers and response to ED diuresis' objects='troponin result,response to furosemide administered in ED' id='activity11.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='compile the clinical and investigation variables required for the acute heart failure risk score' objects='risk score variables,clinical findings,investigation results' id='activity12.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='calculate and record the EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' objects='EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' id='activity12.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='document the final risk-stratification category and disposition decision to guide admission, discharge, or transfer' objects='final risk-stratification record,disposition decision' id='activity1.2'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
    <branch condition='high intensity: ventilation is required and/or ICU admission is required based on high-risk markers or instability' id='branch11'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway4'>
        <branch condition='noninvasive ventilation is required' id='branch12'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate noninvasive ventilation' objects='noninvasive ventilation support' id='activity13'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='invasive ventilation is required' id='branch13'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate invasive ventilation' objects='invasive ventilation support' id='activity14'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway5'>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is required' id='branch14'>
          <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='ICU bed' id='activity15'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is not required immediately' id='branch19'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='continue high-acuity monitoring and treatment while reassessing level-of-care needs' objects='acute heart failure treatment plan,monitoring resources' id='activity22'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is available locally' id='branch15'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='continue high-intensity acute heart failure management at current hospital' objects='critical care resources' id='activity16'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is not available locally' id='branch16'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='transfer patient to a higher level provider through the hospital transfer pathway' objects='transfer mechanism,higher level provider' id='activity17'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='low intensity: EHMRG quintile 1 or 2, patient responded to initial treatment in the ED, and there are no other considerations preventing discharge home' id='branch17'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='provide discharge instructions and return precautions' objects='discharge instructions,return precautions' id='activity19.1'/>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='complete discharge documentation and communicate the follow-up plan' objects='discharge documentation,follow-up plan' id='activity19.2'/>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='arrange follow-up visit with primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED' objects='follow-up appointment' id='activity20'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='average intensity: inpatient admission is required with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch18'>
      <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='inpatient bed' id='activity21'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
</process>

[2026-07-04 19:40:20 INFO] flask app.py did not start for online log
[2026-07-04 19:40:20 INFO] **[Seminar Conclusion]**:

 Problem: The gateway design under the high-intensity branch is unreasonable. In the <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>, the branches "noninvasive ventilation is required" and "invasive ventilation is required" can be selected together, which creates a logic problem.

Suggested modification: Replace the current <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'> with a structure where ventilation choice is exclusive, while ICU admission remains separately selectable. For example, keep ICU admission as a separate activity after the ventilation decision, and change the ventilation choice to an exclusive gateway:

Reason: Noninvasive ventilation and invasive ventilation are alternative respiratory support modalities at a decision point, not parallel or simultaneously selectable interventions. A patient may escalate from noninvasive to invasive ventilation later, but the current structure incorrectly allows both branches to be activated at the same time. ICU admission, however, may occur together with either ventilation option, so it should not be grouped in the same inclusive decision as if all three options were independently co-selectable.

Modified scheme:
<process>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess presenting symptoms, hemodynamic status, and initial acuity using the standardized acute heart failure assessment process' objects='patient with suspected acute heart failure,standardized assessment criteria' id='activity1.1'/>
  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' objects='blood samples' id='activity2'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch2'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure troponin' objects='blood samples' id='activity3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform complete blood count' objects='blood samples' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform electrocardiogram' objects='ECG' id='activity5'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='no recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch7'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform echocardiogram' objects='echocardiogram' id='activity7'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch8'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='review recent echocardiogram' objects='recent echocardiogram results' id='activity8'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch9'>
      <activity role='nursing staff' action='measure heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation frequently until the patient is stabilized' objects='vital signs and oxygen saturation readings' id='activity9'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='administer initial furosemide in the ED and assess responsiveness to diuresis' objects='furosemide,response to diuresis' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess bedside high-risk clinical markers' objects='respiratory distress,hypoxemia,severity of pulmonary edema,hemodynamic status,arrhythmias' id='activity11.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='review investigation-based high-risk markers and response to ED diuresis' objects='troponin result,response to furosemide administered in ED' id='activity11.2'/>

  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='document the final risk-stratification category and disposition decision to guide admission, discharge, or transfer' objects='final risk-stratification record,disposition decision' id='activity1.2'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
    <branch condition='high intensity: ventilation is required and/or ICU admission is required based on high-risk markers or instability' id='branch11'>

        </branch>

        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway5'>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is required' id='branch14'>

| **task_prompt** | Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above). Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure Module 1: Risk Stratification Module 1 Recommended Practices 1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals. 1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis Initial investigations should include: • serum creatinine and electrolyte levels • troponin measurements • complete blood count • electrocardiogram • chest x-ray examination • an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available • frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized 1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED. Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing. High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing. High-risk markers include: • respiratory distress • hypoxemia • severity of pulmonary edema • poor response to furosemide administered in ED • hemodynamic compromise • significant arrhythmias • positive troponin 1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score). As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital. Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence. Module 1 Implementation Considerations General Considerations for Risk Stratification • Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department. • All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider |

[2026-07-04 19:40:20 INFO] Process design reviewer: **[Start Chat]**

[You're a process design expert. Now, we share a common interest in collaborating to successfully complete a task assigned by a new customer.
You are familiar with exclusive gateway, parallel gateway and inclusive gateway in BPMN. Your main responsibilities include process generation, process refinement, process review and so on, guided by team leader.
You model the process of a clinical practice guideline to a clinical pathway.
Here is a new customer's task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider.
To complete the task, I will give you one or more instructions, and you must help me to write a specific solution that appropriately solves the requested instruction based on your expertise and my needs.]

task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider
process text: 
<process>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess presenting symptoms, hemodynamic status, and initial acuity using the standardized acute heart failure assessment process' objects='patient with suspected acute heart failure,standardized assessment criteria' id='activity1.1'/>
  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' objects='blood samples' id='activity2'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch2'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure troponin' objects='blood samples' id='activity3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform complete blood count' objects='blood samples' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform electrocardiogram' objects='ECG' id='activity5'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='no recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch7'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform echocardiogram' objects='echocardiogram' id='activity7'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch8'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='review recent echocardiogram' objects='recent echocardiogram results' id='activity8'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch9'>
      <activity role='nursing staff' action='measure heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation frequently until the patient is stabilized' objects='vital signs and oxygen saturation readings' id='activity9'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='administer initial furosemide in the ED and assess responsiveness to diuresis' objects='furosemide,response to diuresis' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess bedside high-risk clinical markers' objects='respiratory distress,hypoxemia,severity of pulmonary edema,hemodynamic status,arrhythmias' id='activity11.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='review investigation-based high-risk markers and response to ED diuresis' objects='troponin result,response to furosemide administered in ED' id='activity11.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='compile the clinical and investigation variables required for the acute heart failure risk score' objects='risk score variables,clinical findings,investigation results' id='activity12.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='calculate and record the EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' objects='EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' id='activity12.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='document the final risk-stratification category and disposition decision to guide admission, discharge, or transfer' objects='final risk-stratification record,disposition decision' id='activity1.2'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
    <branch condition='high intensity: ventilation is required and/or ICU admission is required based on high-risk markers or instability' id='branch11'>
      <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='noninvasive ventilation is required' id='branch12'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate noninvasive ventilation' objects='noninvasive ventilation support' id='activity13'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='invasive ventilation is required' id='branch13'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate invasive ventilation' objects='invasive ventilation support' id='activity14'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is required' id='branch14'>
          <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='ICU bed' id='activity15'/>
        </branch>
      </inclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is available locally' id='branch15'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='continue high-intensity acute heart failure management at current hospital' objects='critical care resources' id='activity16'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is not available locally' id='branch16'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='transfer patient to a higher level provider through the hospital transfer pathway' objects='transfer mechanism,higher level provider' id='activity17'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='low intensity: EHMRG quintile 1 or 2, patient responded to initial treatment in the ED, and there are no other considerations preventing discharge home' id='branch17'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='provide discharge instructions and return precautions' objects='discharge instructions,return precautions' id='activity19.1'/>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='complete discharge documentation and communicate the follow-up plan' objects='discharge documentation,follow-up plan' id='activity19.2'/>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='arrange follow-up visit with primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED' objects='follow-up appointment' id='activity20'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='average intensity: inpatient admission is required with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch18'>
      <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='inpatient bed' id='activity21'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
</process>
suggestion:
Problem: The gateway design under the high-intensity branch is unreasonable. In the <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>, the branches "noninvasive ventilation is required" and "invasive ventilation is required" can be selected together, which creates a logic problem.

Suggested modification: Replace the current <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'> with a structure where ventilation choice is exclusive, while ICU admission remains separately selectable. For example, keep ICU admission as a separate activity after the ventilation decision, and change the ventilation choice to an exclusive gateway:

Reason: Noninvasive ventilation and invasive ventilation are alternative respiratory support modalities at a decision point, not parallel or simultaneously selectable interventions. A patient may escalate from noninvasive to invasive ventilation later, but the current structure incorrectly allows both branches to be activated at the same time. ICU admission, however, may occur together with either ventilation option, so it should not be grouped in the same inclusive decision as if all three options were independently co-selectable.

Modified scheme:
<process>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess presenting symptoms, hemodynamic status, and initial acuity using the standardized acute heart failure assessment process' objects='patient with suspected acute heart failure,standardized assessment criteria' id='activity1.1'/>
  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' objects='blood samples' id='activity2'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch2'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure troponin' objects='blood samples' id='activity3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform complete blood count' objects='blood samples' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform electrocardiogram' objects='ECG' id='activity5'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='no recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch7'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform echocardiogram' objects='echocardiogram' id='activity7'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch8'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='review recent echocardiogram' objects='recent echocardiogram results' id='activity8'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch9'>
      <activity role='nursing staff' action='measure heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation frequently until the patient is stabilized' objects='vital signs and oxygen saturation readings' id='activity9'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='administer initial furosemide in the ED and assess responsiveness to diuresis' objects='furosemide,response to diuresis' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess bedside high-risk clinical markers' objects='respiratory distress,hypoxemia,severity of pulmonary edema,hemodynamic status,arrhythmias' id='activity11.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='review investigation-based high-risk markers and response to ED diuresis' objects='troponin result,response to furosemide administered in ED' id='activity11.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='compile the clinical and investigation variables required for the acute heart failure risk score' objects='risk score variables,clinical findings,investigation results' id='activity12.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='calculate and record the EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' objects='EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' id='activity12.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='document the final risk-stratification category and disposition decision to guide admission, discharge, or transfer' objects='final risk-stratification record,disposition decision' id='activity1.2'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
    <branch condition='high intensity: ventilation is required and/or ICU admission is required based on high-risk markers or instability' id='branch11'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway4'>
        <branch condition='noninvasive ventilation is required' id='branch12'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate noninvasive ventilation' objects='noninvasive ventilation support' id='activity13'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='invasive ventilation is required' id='branch13'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate invasive ventilation' objects='invasive ventilation support' id='activity14'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway5'>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is required' id='branch14'>
          <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='ICU bed' id='activity15'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is not required immediately' id='branch19'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='continue high-acuity monitoring and treatment while reassessing level-of-care needs' objects='acute heart failure treatment plan,monitoring resources' id='activity22'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is available locally' id='branch15'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='continue high-intensity acute heart failure management at current hospital' objects='critical care resources' id='activity16'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is not available locally' id='branch16'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='transfer patient to a higher level provider through the hospital transfer pathway' objects='transfer mechanism,higher level provider' id='activity17'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='low intensity: EHMRG quintile 1 or 2, patient responded to initial treatment in the ED, and there are no other considerations preventing discharge home' id='branch17'>

You need to modify the process text according to the suggestions and output it.
Be careful not to change the format:

When the process starts, please output '<process>'
An activity is represented as follows:
<activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
The exclusive gateway is represented as follows:
<exclusiveGateway id=''>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</exclusiveGateway>
The parallel gateway is represented as follows:
<parallelGateway id=''>
  <branch id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</parallelGateway>
The inclusive gateway is represented as follows:
<inclusiveGateway id=''>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</inclusiveGateway>
At the end of the process, please output '</process>'

There are a few requirements that should be noted when generating process text:
    1) role, action, objects and id in an activity represent the subject of the activity, the activity behavior, the items needed to perform in the activity and the activity id, respectively.
    2) There are at least two branches within the exclusive gateway and the parallel gateway.
    3) Activity, gateway, and branch id attributes must be unique.
    4) When the branch in the exclusive gateway has a condition, fill the condition into the condition attribute. When the branch unconditional within the exclusive gateway, the condition attribute of the branch =''. However, all branches within a exclusive gateway can only be conditional, or both unconditional.
    5) The branch in the parallel gateway has no condition attribute.
    6) Each branch in the inclusive gateway must have condition.
    7) There must be at least one activity inside the branch tag.
    8) The activity of a sequential relation need not be represented by a gateway, that is, there is no 'sequenceGateway' label.

The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups

Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider.

<process>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess presenting symptoms, hemodynamic status, and initial acuity using the standardized acute heart failure assessment process' objects='patient with suspected acute heart failure,standardized assessment criteria' id='activity1.1'/>
  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' objects='blood samples' id='activity2'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch2'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='measure troponin' objects='blood samples' id='activity3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform complete blood count' objects='blood samples' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform electrocardiogram' objects='ECG' id='activity5'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='no recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch7'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='perform echocardiogram' objects='echocardiogram' id='activity7'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch8'>
          <activity role='clinical staff' action='review recent echocardiogram' objects='recent echocardiogram results' id='activity8'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch9'>
      <activity role='nursing staff' action='measure heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation frequently until the patient is stabilized' objects='vital signs and oxygen saturation readings' id='activity9'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='administer initial furosemide in the ED and assess responsiveness to diuresis' objects='furosemide,response to diuresis' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='assess bedside high-risk clinical markers' objects='respiratory distress,hypoxemia,severity of pulmonary edema,hemodynamic status,arrhythmias' id='activity11.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='review investigation-based high-risk markers and response to ED diuresis' objects='troponin result,response to furosemide administered in ED' id='activity11.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='compile the clinical and investigation variables required for the acute heart failure risk score' objects='risk score variables,clinical findings,investigation results' id='activity12.1'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='calculate and record the EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' objects='EHMRG or equivalent acute heart failure risk score' id='activity12.2'/>
  <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='document the final risk-stratification category and disposition decision to guide admission, discharge, or transfer' objects='final risk-stratification record,disposition decision' id='activity1.2'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
    <branch condition='high intensity: ventilation is required and/or ICU admission is required based on high-risk markers or instability' id='branch11'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway4'>
        <branch condition='noninvasive ventilation is required' id='branch12'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate noninvasive ventilation' objects='noninvasive ventilation support' id='activity13'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='invasive ventilation is required' id='branch13'>
          <activity role='critical care team' action='initiate invasive ventilation' objects='invasive ventilation support' id='activity14'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway5'>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is required' id='branch14'>
          <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='ICU bed' id='activity15'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='ICU admission is not required immediately' id='branch19'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='continue high-acuity monitoring and treatment while reassessing level-of-care needs' objects='acute heart failure treatment plan,monitoring resources' id='activity22'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is available locally' id='branch15'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='continue high-intensity acute heart failure management at current hospital' objects='critical care resources' id='activity16'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='required higher level care is not available locally' id='branch16'>
          <activity role='hospital team' action='transfer patient to a higher level provider through the hospital transfer pathway' objects='transfer mechanism,higher level provider' id='activity17'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='low intensity: EHMRG quintile 1 or 2, patient responded to initial treatment in the ED, and there are no other considerations preventing discharge home' id='branch17'>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='provide discharge instructions and return precautions' objects='discharge instructions,return precautions' id='activity19.1'/>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='complete discharge documentation and communicate the follow-up plan' objects='discharge documentation,follow-up plan' id='activity19.2'/>
      <activity role='emergency department clinician' action='arrange follow-up visit with primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED' objects='follow-up appointment' id='activity20'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='average intensity: inpatient admission is required with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch18'>
      <activity role='admitting team' action='admit patient to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='inpatient bed' id='activity21'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
</process>

| **task_prompt** | Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above). Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure Module 1: Risk Stratification Module 1 Recommended Practices 1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals. 1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis Initial investigations should include: • serum creatinine and electrolyte levels • troponin measurements • complete blood count • electrocardiogram • chest x-ray examination • an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available • frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized 1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED. Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing. High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing. High-risk markers include: • respiratory distress • hypoxemia • severity of pulmonary edema • poor response to furosemide administered in ED • hemodynamic compromise • significant arrhythmias • positive troponin 1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score). As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital. Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence. Module 1 Implementation Considerations General Considerations for Risk Stratification • Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department. • All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider |

In the , the branches "noninvasive ventilation is required" and "invasive ventilation is required" can be selected together, which creates a logic problem.\n\nSuggested modification: Replace the current  with a structure where ventilation choice is exclusive, while ICU admission remains separately selectable. For example, keep ICU admission as a separate activity after the ventilation decision, and change the ventilation choice to an exclusive gateway:\n\nReason: Noninvasive ventilation and invasive ventilation are alternative respiratory support modalities at a decision point, not parallel or simultaneously selectable interventions. A patient may escalate from noninvasive to invasive ventilation later, but the current structure incorrectly allows both branches to be activated at the same time. ICU admission, however, may occur together with either ventilation option, so it should not be grouped in the same inclusive decision as if all three options were independently co-selectable.\n\nModified scheme:\n\n  \n  \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n        \n          \n        \n        \n          \n        \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n  \n  \n  \n  \n  \n  \n  \n    \n      \n        \n          \n        \n        \n          \n        \n      \n      \n        \n          \n        \n        \n          \n        \n      \n      \n        \n          \n        \n        \n          \n        \n      \n    \n    \n      \n      \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n  \n', 'format': "\nWhen the process starts, please output ''\nAn activity is represented as follows:\n\nThe exclusive gateway is represented as follows:\n\n  \n    \n  \n  \n    \n  \n\nThe parallel gateway is represented as follows:\n\n  \n    \n  \n  \n    \n  \n\nThe inclusive gateway is represented as follows:\n\n  \n    \n  \n  \n    \n  \n\nAt the end of the process, please output ''\n\nThere are a few requirements that should be noted when generating process text:\n    1) role, action, objects and id in an activity represent the subject of the activity, the activity behavior, the items needed to perform in the activity and the activity id, respectively.\n    2) There are at least two branches within the exclusive gateway and the parallel gateway.\n    3) Activity, gateway, and branch id attributes must be unique.\n    4) When the branch in the exclusive gateway has a condition, fill the condition into the condition attribute. When the branch unconditional within the exclusive gateway, the condition attribute of the branch =''. However, all branches within a exclusive gateway can only be conditional, or both unconditional.\n    5) The branch in the parallel gateway has no condition attribute.\n    6) Each branch in the inclusive gateway must have condition.\n    7) There must be at least one activity inside the branch tag.\n    8) The activity of a sequential relation need not be represented by a gateway, that is, there is no 'sequenceGateway' label.\n", 'examples': ' '} |

In the , the branches "noninvasive ventilation is required" and "invasive ventilation is required" can be selected together, which creates a logic problem.\n\nSuggested modification: Replace the current  with a structure where ventilation choice is exclusive, while ICU admission remains separately selectable. For example, keep ICU admission as a separate activity after the ventilation decision, and change the ventilation choice to an exclusive gateway:\n\nReason: Noninvasive ventilation and invasive ventilation are alternative respiratory support modalities at a decision point, not parallel or simultaneously selectable interventions. A patient may escalate from noninvasive to invasive ventilation later, but the current structure incorrectly allows both branches to be activated at the same time. ICU admission, however, may occur together with either ventilation option, so it should not be grouped in the same inclusive decision as if all three options were independently co-selectable.\n\nModified scheme:\n\n  \n  \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n        \n          \n        \n        \n          \n        \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n  \n  \n  \n  \n  \n  \n  \n    \n      \n        \n          \n        \n        \n          \n        \n      \n      \n        \n          \n        \n        \n          \n        \n      \n      \n        \n          \n        \n        \n          \n        \n      \n    \n    \n      \n      \n      \n    \n    \n      \n    \n  \n', 'format': "\nWhen the process starts, please output ''\nAn activity is represented as follows:\n\nThe exclusive gateway is represented as follows:\n\n  \n    \n  \n  \n    \n  \n\nThe parallel gateway is represented as follows:\n\n  \n    \n  \n  \n    \n  \n\nThe inclusive gateway is represented as follows:\n\n  \n    \n  \n  \n    \n  \n\nAt the end of the process, please output ''\n\nThere are a few requirements that should be noted when generating process text:\n    1) role, action, objects and id in an activity represent the subject of the activity, the activity behavior, the items needed to perform in the activity and the activity id, respectively.\n    2) There are at least two branches within the exclusive gateway and the parallel gateway.\n    3) Activity, gateway, and branch id attributes must be unique.\n    4) When the branch in the exclusive gateway has a condition, fill the condition into the condition attribute.
